model_phrasing: Model phrasing: GPT-5.x detected from the system harness; Phrasing profile id RTM-PH6OCQ6MX4.
decision_target: Decide whether controller(flow), per-exec tags, awaited promises, a current runtime resource, and explicit ports compose bounded GitHub issue queue workers without start, spawn, or a public pool.
source_of_truth: workers/dkr-4/queue-probe.mjs, workers/dkr-4/queue-contract.json, current Lite flow-handle source and patterns, and the DKR-2 candidate plus its rejected validator record
read_method: Run workers/dkr-4/replay.sh and compare eight queue cases, five explicit ports, one activation per received lease, bounded concurrency, promise joins, checkpoint shape, eight active walls, evidence hashes, and the explicit DKR-2 non-assumption.
questions_answered: A fixed loop can bound admission, apply backpressure with Promise.race, and join every active promise before return and resource cleanup.
Null ends only a bounded source; a long-lived physical receive port blocks until delivery or explicit shutdown, so the watcher needs no polling timer.
Every received lease gets exactly one activation flow execution with per-exec session, authority, issue, queue, lease, safe observation, and probe tracker tags.
Queue receive, acknowledge, reject, and lease validation plus timer wait are five required tag-carried flow ports substituted through createScope.

Zero messages, one message, a five-message burst above concurrency two, handler error, acknowledgement error, lease loss and retry, shutdown while active, and two sessions in one Scope replay at eight of eight.
Lease loss becomes a new received lease attempt; each attempt has its own activation and observation id.
The burst observes concurrency two, records backpressure, and never exceeds the configured bound.
A public pool, start, or spawn API adds no behavior beyond the fixed loop for the replayed cases.
The queue composition works with ordinary awaited promises without assuming DKR-2 acceptance; forced context close remains a separate candidate lifecycle dependency.
questions_unanswered: DKR-2 structured forced-close cancellation remains independently unaccepted and cannot be used as an implementation premise.
A physical queue adapter must define provider-specific acknowledgement idempotency, lease token fencing, and receive shutdown semantics behind the same ports.
Cross-process fairness and distributed worker ownership belong to the physical queue, not this in-process bounded composition.
decision: candidate only; controller(flow) plus ordinary awaited promises is sufficient for bounded queue composition, with no start, spawn, public pool, or new Lite primitive supported by this worker

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Candidate checkpoint with DKR-2 unaccepted and independent DKR-4 replay pending

wall_gate.reason: The DKR-4 replay is writer-only, and DKR-2 remains an unaccepted separate lifecycle dependency.

confidence_probability_update: 0.52 before composition probes; 0.91 after eight queue cases, five explicit port edges, bounded concurrency two, thirteen lease activations, and zero added pool behavior
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: The flow graph owns orchestration while physical queue and timer effects remain required scope-substitutable ports.
One activation exec per lease keeps session and authority tags activation-local even when two sessions share a server Scope.
A pool abstraction adds no behavior to the bounded loop and would create unsupported public lifecycle surface.
Normal and delivery-triggered shutdown need only awaited promises; forced context close remains conditional on later DKR-2 acceptance.
No real external service was accessed and no product source changed.

candidate_ckrs.target: 8/8 cases with max concurrency 2, active promises 0 after join, and one activation exec per lease
5 explicit ports with undeclared_effect_edge_count 0 and scope_seam_escape_count 0

candidate_pkrs.scope: Define queue receive, acknowledge, reject, lease validation, and timer wait adapter tags for the issue-triage example.
Compose the bounded watch, delivery, and activation flows with a current runtime resource and awaited promises.
Carry the eight deterministic queue cases into public scope-seam example tests after DKR acceptance.

checkpoint_rejection_policy: Superficial, tautological, false-positive, fabricated, unsupported, or single-LLM-truth checkpoints are rejected. Missing, stale, wrong-source, non-replayable, or contradicted evidence fails closed. An empty result remains valid only with a replayable probe trace.
role_grammar: CKRs are measurable contribution context, not worker work.
DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
